title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston is the most collision-prone city in the U.S., with drivers averaging 3.7 years between crashes.
Worcester and Springfield also rank low in Allstate's report, with drivers experiencing collisions every 5.14 and 5.18 years, respectively.
Providence, RI ranks among the top 10 riskiest cities, while Brownsville, TX has the lowest collision rate, with drivers averaging 15 years between crashes.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, led police on a wrong-way, high-speed chase over the Bourne Bridge on Thursday.
The chase began after Deleon swerved into a breakdown lane during a medical emergency on Route 25.
Deleon faces multiple charges and is held without bail; he's due back in Falmouth District Court on Aug. 5.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police released new surveillance photos of a suspect in a lemonade stand robbery in South Boston.
A 14-year-old was previously arrested for the June 10 robbery; he faces armed robbery and firearm possession charges.
Community support rose for the stand, with Mayor Wu attending and proceeds aiding a gun violence prevention group.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is considering his next NBA team, with Boston Celtics as a possible but less likely destination.
Rich Paul mentioned 'Brad + Tatum' as a potential landing spot for James, but not among top contenders.
Celtics' lineup, if James joins, could include Derrick White, Paul George, Mitchell Robinson, Tatum, and James.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticized the Celtics for trading Jaylen Brown to the 76ers, calling the decision disturbing.
Parish compared the Brown trade to the controversial Luka Doncic trade by the Mavericks in 2025.
Despite his objections, Parish hopes Jaylen Brown's contributions will be honored by the Celtics in the future.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed gratitude to former teammate Jaylen Brown after Brown was traded to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Jaylen Brown was traded for Paul George and multiple draft picks, following nine seasons and a 2024 NBA Finals win with Tatum.
Brown expressed discontent over the trade process, mentioning lack of respect and unforeseen departure from the Celtics.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics extend center Neemias Queta with a four-year, $56 million contract, securing him through the 2030-31 season.
Queta averaged 10.2 points, 8.4 rebounds, and 1.3 blocks per game in the 2025-26 season, starting 75 times.
The agreement follows a major roster change, including the trade of Jaylen Brown and signing of Mitchell Robinson.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates 200 years as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
The building, a National Historic Landmark, dates back to 1716 or 1717, rich with Revolutionary-era history.
Owner Joseph Milano increased capacity to 550, maintaining original menu items and serving 1,000-1,500 guests daily.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston's eight-day event spree starts July 4 with the Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular featuring performances by Chance the Rapper and others.
On July 9, Gillette Stadium will host a World Cup quarterfinal, marking Boston's last match of the tournament.
More than 60 tall ships will sail into Boston Harbor on July 11 during Sail Boston's Parade of Sail to celebrate America’s 250th anniversary.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House, established in 1826, is America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
Six U.S. presidents, including JFK and Barack Obama, have dined at Boston's Union Oyster House.
Joseph Milano commissioned a painting in 2003 depicting famous patrons like Al Pacino and Meryl Streep.
